column1: Content: project description and value proposition
column6: One-sentence description is clear and specific; the problem being solved and why it matters are communicated concisely for the target audience; the "so what?" is compelling and non-generic; content is written for a real audience, not for a grader.
column9: Description and value proposition are present but may be vague, technically-focused rather than audience-focused, or written primarily for an evaluator rather than a real audience.
column12: Project description is missing, too vague to understand what the project does, or reads like a course deliverable rather than a real page.

column6: 3–5 features, findings, or capabilities described from the audience's perspective (not the implementation perspective); at least one screenshot, screen recording, figure, or embedded demo shows the project in action or illustrates results; media is legible, purposeful, and directly illustrates the claims made.
column9: Features or findings are listed; media is present but may be low quality, off-topic, or fails to clearly demonstrate functionality or results.
column12: Features or media are missing; or content is described only in technical terms with no audience framing.

column1: Call to action and how to access
column6: A clear, prominent call to action (Download, Play, Try it, View on GitHub, Read the paper, etc.) is present and functional; prerequisites and platform limitations are briefly noted; the path from the page to actually engaging with the project is obvious.
column9: A call to action exists but may be buried, non-functional, or missing important prerequisites or limitations.
column12: No clear call to action; or the link is broken; or it is not clear how to access or engage with the project.

column6: Page looks intentional and professional; visual hierarchy guides the reader; typography, spacing, and layout are consistent; the page would not be embarrassing to share in a job interview or with a real user; platform conventions are followed appropriately.
column9: Page is functional and readable but feels unpolished or like a default template with minimal customization.
column12: Page appears unfinished, uses broken layout, or looks like a rough draft.

column6: All current team members are credited; past contributors are acknowledged if this is a multi-year project; GitHub or portfolio links are included for each member (or deliberately omitted with a stated reason); a contact or feedback channel is present.
column9: Team credits are present but some members are missing, past contributors are unacknowledged on a multi-year project, or no contact channel is provided.
column12: Team credits or contact information are missing.

column6: All images have alt text; color contrast is sufficient for readability; links use descriptive text; videos or GIFs have captions or a written description; the page is navigable without relying solely on color or visual layout.
column9: Some accessibility practices are used; minor gaps (e.g., a few images missing alt text, or link text could be more descriptive).
column12: Missing alt text throughout; insufficient color contrast; or the page is not meaningfully accessible.
